name: Andhra Pradesh
stopoverNote: Andhra Pradesh is large: fly into Visakhapatnam, use train to Vijayawada, then a hired car for the Vijayawada→Gandikota→Tirupati arc; the Vijayawada→Gandikota leg is the trip's long, public-transport-light stretch and should not be paired with sightseeing the same day.

diet.vegetarian: Andhra Pradesh is very vegetarian-friendly: temple towns, tiffin houses and Andhra meals serve rice, pappu, sambar, rasam, vegetable curries, gongura pachadi, pesarattu and pulihora. Tirupati and Tirumala are especially pure-vegetarian; expect very spicy food and ask clearly for less chilli.
diet.vegan: Vegan travel is manageable with care. Pulihora, coconut rice, many chutneys and street snacks can be vegan, but ask for no ghee, butter, milk or curd. Tirumala laddu prasadam and many sweets contain ghee, and some temple meals use ghee, so confirm before eating.
phrases: Kaaram takkuva cheyyandi = please make it less spicy
Neyyi, paalu, perugu vaddu = no ghee, milk or curd
Naku shakaharam kavali = I want vegetarian food

cities.name: Visakhapatnam
cities.notes: Andhra's largest coastal city — beaches, naval history, hilltop sea views and the gateway to the Araku Valley mountain railway.

cities.name: Vijayawada & Amaravati
cities.notes: Krishna River hub pairing Kanaka Durga Temple, Prakasam Barrage, Undavalli Caves and Amaravati's ancient Buddhist stupa landscape.

cities.name: Gandikota
cities.notes: A 13th-century fort and sandstone gorge above the Pennar River — Andhra's 'Grand Canyon' and the route's rugged overnight stop.

cities.name: Tirupati
cities.notes: Pilgrim heart of Andhra Pradesh, anchored by Tirumala's Sri Venkateswara Temple and nearby Vijayanagara-era heritage.

cities.name: Lepakshi
cities.notes: Compact Vijayanagara masterpiece near the Karnataka border, known for frescoed pillars, a hanging pillar and a giant Nandi monolith.

itinerary.sim: Jio and Airtel both work well in Visakhapatnam, Vijayawada and Tirupati; coverage can thin out around Araku, Gandikota and Lepakshi, so download maps and carry cash before leaving the cities.

Visakhapatnam.name: Visakhapatnam
Visakhapatnam.note: Two days covers the beach city and Araku as a long excursion; add a third if you want to overnight in Araku instead of rushing the train day.

days.theme: Beach promenade, naval history & hilltop views
days.note: The coast sights sit close together; check INS Kursura's Monday closure and keep Rushikonda water sports before late afternoon if seas are calm.

activities.name: RK Beach sunrise walk and Beach Road
activities.tip: Go early or at golden hour; avoid swimming in rough surf.

activities.name: INS Kursura Submarine Museum
activities.tip: Closed Mondays; the tight submarine interior is the memorable part.

activities.name: Kailasagiri Hill Park and ropeway views
activities.tip: Weekends bring ropeway queues; afternoon light is best for the coastline.

activities.name: Rushikonda Beach water-sports window
activities.tip: Activity availability depends on sea conditions and season.

days.theme: Araku Valley mountain train & Borra Caves
days.note: This is a 12–14 hour day if returning to Vizag. Book the Vistadome train early, start before dawn, and consider overnighting in Araku for a calmer pace.

activities.name: Vistadome train Visakhapatnam → Araku
activities.tip: Book on IRCTC 60–90 days ahead; train 08551 is the signature scenic ride through tunnels and bridges.

activities.name: Borra Caves
activities.tip: Hire a local jeep or auto from Araku; caves can be slippery, so wear proper shoes.

activities.name: Araku Tribal Museum and Padmapuram Gardens
activities.tip: Do this before Borra if you arrive by late morning.

activities.name: Araku organic coffee tasting
activities.tip: Carry cash; smaller cafes may not reliably accept cards or UPI.

hotels.name: Araku overnight guesthouse if slowing down

meals: Late dinner back in Vizag or Araku

days.theme: Buffer day — Simhachalam and quiet beaches
days.note: Use this day if you overnighted in Araku, need cyclone/rail buffer, or want a gentler Vizag finish before the train south.

activities.name: Return from Araku by train or road
activities.tip: Morning return keeps the afternoon free.

activities.name: Simhachalam Narasimha Temple
activities.tip: Visit early morning or evening for cooler weather and shorter queues.

activities.name: Yarada Beach photo stop
activities.tip: Beautiful but not ideal for swimming because of currents.

meals: Andhra meals before boarding the Vijayawada train

Vijayawada & Amaravati.name: Vijayawada & Amaravati
Vijayawada & Amaravati.note: A compact Krishna River and heritage stop: one arrival evening for Kanaka Durga, one half-day circuit for Amaravati and Undavalli.

days.theme: Krishna riverfront and Kanaka Durga darshan
days.note: Arrive by midday from Vizag if possible. Evening darshan is atmospheric, but paid queues and temple timing still need buffer.

activities.name: Kanaka Durga Temple on Indrakeeladri Hill
activities.tip: Paid darshan tiers exist, but verify current rates before booking; dress modestly.

activities.name: Prakasam Barrage dusk walk
activities.tip: Pair it with the temple base; city lights reflect beautifully on the Krishna River.

activities.name: Bhavani Island boat and gardens
activities.tip: Skip if arrival is late because boat timings can close early.

meals: Local sweets such as kaja or Bandar halwa

days.theme: Amaravati Buddhist circuit and Undavalli Caves
days.note: Leave Vijayawada early; Amaravati is roughly an hour away and the museum/stupa schedule can be affected by weekly closures.

activities.name: Amaravati Mahachaitya Buddhist stupa
activities.tip: A guide adds context for the 2nd-century BCE Buddhist remains.

activities.name: Amaravati Archaeological Site Museum
activities.tip: Visit before the stupa for context; confirm Friday closure locally.

activities.name: Undavalli Caves
activities.tip: The rock-cut Vishnu shrine works well on the return from Amaravati.

activities.name: Vijayawada food stop
activities.tip: Ghee is common in sweets, so vegan travellers should ask carefully.

hotels.name: Vijayawada hotel before early Gandikota start

Gandikota.name: Gandikota
Gandikota.note: Basic but spectacular. Arrive by early afternoon for the fort and sunset; stay overnight for sunrise before driving to Tirupati.

days.theme: Gorge, fort ruins and canyon sunset
days.note: Depart Vijayawada very early. The road day is long, facilities are basic, and sunset at the gorge is the non-negotiable anchor.

activities.name: Gandikota Gorge viewpoint over the Pennar River
activities.tip: Reach 30 minutes before sunset and stay away from cliff edges.

activities.name: Gandikota Fort ruins, temples and Jama Masjid
activities.tip: Wear sturdy shoes for uneven stone paths.

activities.name: Overnight camping or APTDC stay and stargazing
activities.tip: Book limited rooms or tents early for weekends; carry cash, water and a torch.

activities.name: Belum Caves detour en route
activities.tip: Only feasible if leaving Vijayawada around 6am; otherwise skip to protect the sunset.

days.theme: Sunrise at the canyon and road to Tirupati
days.note: Treat this as a dawn buffer rather than a full sightseeing day; leave by 8–9am to keep the Tirupati arrival easy.

activities.name: Gandikota sunrise viewpoint
activities.tip: The gorge glows amber at dawn and is quieter than sunset.

activities.name: Breakfast and road departure to Tirupati
activities.tip: Use the same hired taxi; public transport is too slow for this route.

Tirupati.name: Tirupati
Tirupati.note: Book Tirumala darshan before locking dates. Even paid darshan needs hours of logistics, strict dress code and early starts.

days.note: Arrive from Gandikota by noon if possible. Keep the evening light and sleep early if Tirumala is scheduled before dawn.

activities.name: Chandragiri Fort and Raja Mahal Museum
activities.tip: Confirm weekly closure before going; it is an easy taxi ride from Tirupati.

activities.name: Kapila Theertham waterfall temple
activities.tip: A gentle evening stop at the base of the Tirumala hills.

activities.name: TTD ticket, ID, dress-code and cloak-room prep
activities.tip: Keep Aadhaar or accepted original ID for every adult and avoid carrying restricted items uphill.

hotels.name: Tirupati town hotel near bus/rail access

days.theme: Sri Venkateswara Temple darshan at Tirumala
days.note: Pre-book the ₹300 Special Entry Darshan if possible; even then allow 4–6 hours total for uphill travel, queues, darshan and prasadam. Free SSD tokens can mean much longer waits.

activities.name: Sri Venkateswara Swamy Darshan at Tirumala
activities.cost: ₹300/person Special Entry Darshan; free SSD token with longer queue; laddu prasadam ₹50/piece
activities.tip: The inner sanctum view is brief; book online, carry original ID, follow dress code and expect strict no-photography rules.

activities.name: Swami Pushkarini and Tirumala hill surroundings
activities.tip: Do this after darshan only if the group still has energy.

activities.name: Srikalahasti Temple afternoon extension
activities.tip: Skip if darshan queues ran long or travellers are tired.

Lepakshi.name: Lepakshi
Lepakshi.note: Best as a half-day architectural finale from Tirupati or on the onward road to Bangalore; facilities are limited in the village.

days.theme: Vijayanagara frescoes, hanging pillar and Nandi
days.note: Start early from Tirupati to avoid heat. The complex is compact, but the one-way drive is long enough that same-day return or onward flight timing must be protected.

activities.name: Veerabhadra Temple murals, hanging pillar and Nagalinga
activities.tip: Hire a local guide if available; fresco details and pillar legends are easy to miss.

activities.name: Giant Nandi monolith
activities.tip: It sits a short walk from the main temple and is one of the site's best photo stops.

activities.name: Unfinished Kalyana Mandapam columns
activities.tip: Visit before midday for cooler stone floors and better light.

activities.name: Continue to Tirupati airport or Bangalore exit
activities.tip: Bangalore has more flights but adds another long road leg.

hotels.name: Return to Tirupati or continue to Bangalore

meals: Eat in Hindupur or back in Tirupati

connections.method: Train (Vande Bharat or express) or short flight
Road by hired taxi or self-drive; optional Belum Caves detour
Road by the same hired taxi
Road day trip via Hindupur, or continue onward toward Bangalore

extras: Book Tirumala darshan before finalising the route; ₹300 Special Entry Darshan quotas open months ahead and can sell out quickly.
Book the Visakhapatnam→Araku Vistadome train early for Oct–Feb weekends; the Araku day is the easiest place to lose the itinerary if train tickets sell out.
The coast can face Bay of Bengal cyclone disruption in Oct–Dec; inland legs are less affected, but monitor alerts before Vizag and train segments.
Gandikota, Araku and Lepakshi have limited cash and food options; carry water, snacks, downloaded maps and enough cash.
April–June heat is severe across the route, especially Gandikota and Vijayawada, so keep this itinerary to Oct–Mar whenever possible.
